---
title: "Validating BonQuery's Data Against the City of Toronto"
description: "A transparency check comparing BonQuery's Toronto shelter occupancy figures against the City of Toronto's live dashboard, with a full account of known data differences."
author: "Miriam Marling"
open-graph:
  title: "Validating BonQuery's Data Against the City of Toronto"
  description: "A transparency check comparing BonQuery's Toronto shelter occupancy figures against the City of Toronto's live dashboard, with a full account of known data differences."
  site_name: "BonQuery — Data for the Greater Good"
twitter-card:
  card-style: summary
  title: "Validating BonQuery's Data Against the City of Toronto"
  description: "A transparency check comparing BonQuery's Toronto shelter occupancy figures against the City of Toronto's live dashboard, with a full account of known data differences."
---

This page is a transparency check. It places BonQuery's open-data figures for daily shelter occupancy directly beside the City of Toronto's published live-dashboard figures for the same date, and gives a precise account of every difference. BonQuery's table is built entirely from the City's own open data (CKAN export); the live dashboard draws on the City's internal operational system. Most figures match exactly. Where they don't, the reason is documented below — neither source is wrong; they answer slightly different questions.

**Comparison reference date: May 14, 2026.** The City side of this table is a one-time snapshot captured from the live dashboard on May 14, which is no longer visible on the City's site.

---

## Legend

| Mark | Meaning |
|---|---|
| ✓ | **Matches** — BonQuery's open-data figure equals the City's published figure |
| ≈ | **Known difference** — figures differ by a documented, explained amount (see notes below) |
| ✗ | **Unexplained discrepancy** — should never appear; if it does, investigate |

Rows labelled *Not published to open data* are absent from the CKAN export entirely and receive no mark.

---

## Side-by-side comparison — May 14, 2026

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
daily_occ = FileAttachment("../data/daily_occupancy.json").json()
city_ref  = FileAttachment("../data/city_reference_2026-05-14.json").json()
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// BonQuery rows for May 14
bq14 = daily_occ.filter(r => r.date === "2026-05-14")

function bq(key) { return bq14.find(r => r.key === key) }
function cr(key) { return city_ref.find(r => r.key === key) }

function n(v)   { return v !== null && v !== undefined ? (+v).toLocaleString() : "—" }
function pct(v) { return v !== null && v !== undefined ? (+v).toFixed(1) + "%" : "—" }

// Known-difference keys (capacity/rate only — ind and occ match)
KNOWN_CAP_KEYS = new Set(["mix_emerg","men_emerg","wom_emerg","yth_emerg","emerg_total","singles_total"])

function mark(bqVal, cityVal, isCapOrRate, key) {
  if (cityVal === null || cityVal === undefined) return ""
  if (bqVal === null || bqVal === undefined) return ""
  const match = Math.abs(+bqVal - +cityVal) < 0.11
  if (match) return `<span style="color:#1a7a4a;font-weight:bold">✓</span>`
  if (isCapOrRate && KNOWN_CAP_KEYS.has(key))
    return `<span style="color:#b06000;font-weight:bold" title="Known difference — see notes">≈</span>`
  return `<span style="color:#c0392b;font-weight:bold">✗</span>`
}

function compRow(key, bqRow, cityRow, opts = {}) {
  const { indent = 0, isTot = false, summaryOnly = false } = opts
  const pad = indent * 16
  const bold = isTot ? "font-weight:600" : ""
  const bg   = isTot ? "#e8edf5" : "#fff"

  if (!bqRow || !cityRow) return ""

  const useCap = bqRow.col_type !== "summary"

  if (summaryOnly || bqRow.col_type === "summary") {
    return `<tr style="background:${bg}">
      <td style="padding:5px 10px 5px ${pad}px;${bold}">${bqRow.label}</td>
      <td style="padding:5px 8px;text-align:right;${bold}">
        ${n(bqRow.ind)} ${mark(bqRow.ind, cityRow.city_ind, false, key)}</td>
      <td style="padding:5px 8px;text-align:right;color:#888">${n(cityRow.city_ind)}</td>
      <td colspan="6"></td>
    </tr>`
  }

  return `<tr style="background:${bg}">
    <td style="padding:5px 10px 5px ${pad}px;${bold}">${bqRow.label}</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 8px;text-align:right;${bold}">
      ${n(bqRow.ind)} ${mark(bqRow.ind, cityRow.city_ind, false, key)}</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 8px;text-align:right;color:#888">${n(cityRow.city_ind)}</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 8px;text-align:right">
      ${n(bqRow.occ)} ${mark(bqRow.occ, cityRow.city_occ, false, key)}</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 8px;text-align:right;color:#888">${n(cityRow.city_occ)}</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 8px;text-align:right">
      ${n(bqRow.unocc)} ${mark(bqRow.unocc, cityRow.city_unocc, true, key)}</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 8px;text-align:right;color:#888">${n(cityRow.city_unocc)}</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 8px;text-align:right">
      ${n(bqRow.cap)}${bqRow.cap_funding != null && bqRow.cap_funding !== bqRow.cap ? ` <span style="color:#888;font-size:0.85em">(funded ${n(bqRow.cap_funding)})</span>` : ""} ${mark(bqRow.cap, cityRow.city_cap, true, key)}</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 8px;text-align:right;color:#888">${n(cityRow.city_cap)}</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 8px;text-align:right">
      ${pct(bqRow.rate)} ${mark(bqRow.rate, cityRow.city_rate, true, key)}</td>
    <td style="padding:5px 8px;text-align:right;color:#888">${pct(cityRow.city_rate)}</td>
  </tr>`
}

function secHead(label, col1) {
  return `<tr style="background:#1a3c6b;color:white;font-weight:600;font-size:0.82em">
    <td style="padding:6px 10px" rowspan="2">${label}</td>
    <td style="padding:6px 8px;text-align:center" colspan="2">Individuals</td>
    <td style="padding:6px 8px;text-align:center" colspan="2">${col1[0]}</td>
    <td style="padding:6px 8px;text-align:center" colspan="2">${col1[1]}</td>
    <td style="padding:6px 8px;text-align:center" colspan="2">${col1[2]}</td>
    <td style="padding:6px 8px;text-align:center" colspan="2">Occupancy Rate</td>
  </tr>
  <tr style="background:#2c5282;color:#cfe0f7;font-size:0.78em">
    <td style="padding:3px 8px;text-align:center">BQ</td>
    <td style="padding:3px 8px;text-align:center">City</td>
    <td style="padding:3px 8px;text-align:center">BQ</td>
    <td style="padding:3px 8px;text-align:center">City</td>
    <td style="padding:3px 8px;text-align:center">BQ</td>
    <td style="padding:3px 8px;text-align:center">City</td>
    <td style="padding:3px 8px;text-align:center">BQ</td>
    <td style="padding:3px 8px;text-align:center">City</td>
    <td style="padding:3px 8px;text-align:center">BQ</td>
    <td style="padding:3px 8px;text-align:center">City</td>
  </tr>`
}

function summarySecHead() {
  return `<tr style="background:#1a3c6b;color:white;font-weight:600;font-size:0.82em">
    <td style="padding:6px 10px" rowspan="2">Occupancy Summary</td>
    <td style="padding:6px 8px;text-align:center" colspan="2">Individuals</td>
    <td colspan="8"></td>
  </tr>
  <tr style="background:#2c5282;color:#cfe0f7;font-size:0.78em">
    <td style="padding:3px 8px;text-align:center">BQ</td>
    <td style="padding:3px 8px;text-align:center">City</td>
    <td colspan="8"></td>
  </tr>`
}

bridgingRow = `<tr style="background:#f5f5f5;color:#888">
  <td style="padding:5px 10px 5px 20px;font-style:italic">Bridging &amp; Triage Programs</td>
  <td style="padding:5px 8px;text-align:right;font-size:0.85em" colspan="2">Not published to open data (29 people)</td>
  <td colspan="8"></td>
</tr>`

compTable = {
  const rows = [
    "all_shelter","room_based","singles_sector","singles_shelter","allied_summ","temp_summ","iso_summ"
  ]
  return html`
  <div style="overflow-x:auto;margin:1rem 0">
  <table style="border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:0.83em;font-family:'Segoe UI',Arial,sans-serif;min-width:800px">

    ${summarySecHead()}
    ${compRow("all_shelter",     bq("all_shelter"),    cr("all_shelter"),    {isTot:true,  summaryOnly:true})}
    ${bridgingRow}
    ${compRow("room_based",      bq("room_based"),     cr("room_based"),     {indent:1, summaryOnly:true})}
    ${compRow("singles_sector",  bq("singles_sector"), {city_ind:5727},      {indent:1, summaryOnly:true})}
    ${compRow("singles_shelter", bq("singles_shelter"),cr("singles_total"),  {indent:2, summaryOnly:true})}
    ${compRow("allied_summ",     bq("allied_summ"),    cr("allied_total"),   {indent:2, summaryOnly:true})}
    ${compRow("temp_summ",       bq("temp_summ"),      {city_ind:1901},      {indent:2, summaryOnly:true})}
    ${compRow("iso_summ",        bq("iso_summ"),       cr("iso"),            {indent:1, summaryOnly:true})}

    ${secHead("Shelter Programs, Room-Based", ["Occupied Rooms","Unoccupied Rooms","Actual Room Capacity"])}
    ${compRow("fam_total",   bq("fam_total"),   cr("fam_total"),   {isTot:true})}
    ${compRow("fam_emerg",   bq("fam_emerg"),   cr("fam_emerg"),   {indent:1})}
    ${compRow("fam_trans_r", bq("fam_trans_r"), cr("fam_trans_r"), {indent:1})}
    ${compRow("fam_hotel",   bq("fam_hotel"),   cr("fam_hotel"),   {indent:1})}
    ${compRow("sng_hotel",   bq("sng_hotel"),   cr("sng_hotel"),   {isTot:true})}

    ${secHead("Shelter Programs, Bed-Based", ["Occupied Beds","Unoccupied Beds","Actual Bed Capacity"])}
    ${compRow("singles_total", bq("singles_total"), cr("singles_total"), {isTot:true})}
    ${compRow("emerg_total",   bq("emerg_total"),   cr("emerg_total"),   {indent:1, isTot:true})}
    ${compRow("mix_emerg",     bq("mix_emerg"),     cr("mix_emerg"),     {indent:2})}
    ${compRow("men_emerg",     bq("men_emerg"),     cr("men_emerg"),     {indent:2})}
    ${compRow("wom_emerg",     bq("wom_emerg"),     cr("wom_emerg"),     {indent:2})}
    ${compRow("yth_emerg",     bq("yth_emerg"),     cr("yth_emerg"),     {indent:2})}
    ${compRow("trans_total",   bq("trans_total"),   cr("trans_total"),   {indent:1, isTot:true})}
    ${compRow("mix_trans",     bq("mix_trans"),     cr("mix_trans"),     {indent:2})}
    ${compRow("fam_trans_b",   bq("fam_trans_b"),   cr("fam_trans_b"),   {indent:2})}
    ${compRow("men_trans",     bq("men_trans"),     cr("men_trans"),     {indent:2})}
    ${compRow("wom_trans",     bq("wom_trans"),     cr("wom_trans"),     {indent:2})}
    ${compRow("yth_trans",     bq("yth_trans"),     cr("yth_trans"),     {indent:2})}

    ${secHead("Allied Services", ["Occupied Spaces","Unoccupied Spaces","Actual Space Capacity"])}
    ${compRow("allied_total", bq("allied_total"), cr("allied_total"), {isTot:true})}
    ${compRow("respites",     bq("respites"),     cr("respites"),     {indent:1})}
    ${compRow("dropin",       bq("dropin"),       cr("dropin"),       {indent:1})}

    ${secHead("Temporary Programs (Bed/Space)", ["Occ. Beds/Spaces","Unocc. Beds/Spaces","Actual Capacity"])}
    ${compRow("temp_resp", bq("temp_resp"), cr("temp_resp"))}

    ${secHead("Temporary Programs (Room/Unit)", ["Occupied Rooms","Unoccupied Rooms","Actual Room Capacity"])}
    ${compRow("hotels", bq("hotels"), cr("hotels"))}

    ${secHead("Temporary Isolation/Recovery", ["Occupied Rooms","Unoccupied Rooms","Actual Room Capacity"])}
    ${compRow("iso", bq("iso"), cr("iso"))}

  </table>
  </div>`
}
```

---

## Notes on differences

### Emergency bed capacity (≈ mark)

The ≈ mark appears in the **Unoccupied Beds**, **Actual Bed Capacity**, and **Occupancy Rate** cells for emergency bed programs (Mixed Adult, Men, Women, Youth emergency; and their totals). BonQuery's table also shows the funded figure inline next to the actual figure (in grey, parenthesised), so the offline-bed count is reconstructible from the BonQuery data alone: `funded − actual = offline`.

This is not an error in either source — it reflects three different capacity definitions sitting side by side in the table above:

- **BonQuery's "Actual Bed Capacity"** (the figure under the BQ column): beds physically set up and bookable for service users on the date of recording. The CKAN data dictionary explicitly notes this excludes beds taken out of service for **maintenance, repair, or service users in self-isolation**.
- **BonQuery's "(funded N)"** (shown in grey next to the actual figure): the contractually funded number of beds — what the program is committed by funding agreement to operate.
- **City's "Actual Bed Capacity"** (the figure under the City column): reports a capacity figure that generally falls between BonQuery's Actual Bed Capacity and the funded figure, and is typically close to — but not identical to — the funded number. For at least one program type (Mixed Adult, Emergency), the City's dashboard figure does not align with either of the BonQuery definitions. The exact derivation of the City's "Actual Bed Capacity" on the live dashboard is not publicly documented.

Offline beds aren't phantoms: each one is a real, contracted bed that the shelter operator has taken out of service for the day. The reasons documented in the CKAN data dictionary are maintenance, repair, and isolation; other reasons that have been reported publicly in Toronto's shelter system include staffing shortages and pest control. The operator records the offline status program-by-program; the City compiles and publishes the figures.

BonQuery's "Actual Bed Capacity" column uses the CKAN open-data definition because that is the published, reproducible figure; the funded figure is shown alongside it for transparency.

### Reconstructing the gap, program by program

The breakdown below isolates the emergency bed programs row by row. For each program it shows the three capacity figures from the comparison table above and two computed gaps. The **City − BQ Gap** column shows how far the City's dashboard figure sits from BonQuery's Actual Bed Capacity — the discrepancy this page sets out to explain. The **Offline Beds** column shows the count of beds the program is funded to operate but has currently taken out of service (for maintenance, repair, isolation, staffing shortages, or pest control, as described above); it is computed as funded capacity minus Actual Bed Capacity.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
breakdownTable = {
  const keys = ["men_emerg","wom_emerg","mix_emerg","yth_emerg","emerg_total","singles_total"]
  const rows = keys.map(k => {
    const b = bq(k)
    const c = cr(k)
    if (!b || !c) return null
    const funded   = b.cap_funding
    const cityGap  = (c.city_cap !== null && c.city_cap !== undefined && b.cap !== null) ? c.city_cap - b.cap : null
    const offline  = (funded !== null && funded !== undefined && b.cap !== null) ? funded - b.cap : null
    return {key: k, label: b.label, occ: b.occ, bq_cap: b.cap, city_cap: c.city_cap, funded, cityGap, offline}
  }).filter(Boolean)

  function signed(v) {
    if (v === null || v === undefined) return "—"
    if (v === 0) return "0"
    return (v > 0 ? "+" : "") + (+v).toLocaleString()
  }

  return html`<table style="border-collapse:collapse;margin:1em 0;font-size:0.88em;width:100%;max-width:780px">
    <thead>
      <tr style="background:#1a3c6b;color:white;font-weight:600;font-size:0.95em">
        <th style="padding:8px 10px;text-align:left">Program</th>
        <th style="padding:8px 8px;text-align:right">Occupied Beds</th>
        <th style="padding:8px 8px;text-align:right">Actual Bed Capacity<br><span style="font-weight:400;font-size:0.82em;color:#cfe0f7">BonQuery</span></th>
        <th style="padding:8px 8px;text-align:right">Actual Bed Capacity<br><span style="font-weight:400;font-size:0.82em;color:#cfe0f7">City dashboard</span></th>
        <th style="padding:8px 8px;text-align:right">Funded Capacity<br><span style="font-weight:400;font-size:0.82em;color:#cfe0f7">BonQuery</span></th>
        <th style="padding:8px 8px;text-align:right">City − BQ Gap</th>
        <th style="padding:8px 8px;text-align:right">Offline Beds</th>
      </tr>
    </thead>
    <tbody>
      ${rows.map(r => {
        const isTotal = r.key === "emerg_total" || r.key === "singles_total"
        const bg   = isTotal ? "#e8edf5" : "#fff"
        const bold = isTotal ? "font-weight:600" : ""
        const gapColor     = r.cityGap !== null && r.cityGap !== 0 ? "color:#b06000" : ""
        const offlineColor = r.offline !== null && r.offline !== 0 ? "color:#b06000" : ""
        return html`<tr style="background:${bg};${bold}">
          <td style="padding:6px 10px">${r.label}</td>
          <td style="padding:6px 8px;text-align:right">${n(r.occ)} <span style="color:#1a7a4a;font-weight:bold">✓</span></td>
          <td style="padding:6px 8px;text-align:right">${n(r.bq_cap)}</td>
          <td style="padding:6px 8px;text-align:right">${n(r.city_cap)}</td>
          <td style="padding:6px 8px;text-align:right">${n(r.funded)}</td>
          <td style="padding:6px 8px;text-align:right;${gapColor}">${signed(r.cityGap)}</td>
          <td style="padding:6px 8px;text-align:right;${offlineColor}">${signed(r.offline)}</td>
        </tr>`
      })}
    </tbody>
  </table>`
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
mixEmergAnomaly = {
  const m = bq("mix_emerg")
  const mc = cr("mix_emerg")
  if (!m || !mc || mc.city_cap == null || m.cap == null || m.cap_funding == null) return md``
  const city   = mc.city_cap
  const actual = m.cap
  const funded = m.cap_funding
  // Build a short, factual call-out describing whichever shape the anomaly takes.
  let sentence
  if (city > funded) {
    sentence = `The Mixed Adult Emergency row is notable: the City's dashboard shows **${n(city)}** beds, which is **${n(city - funded)}** above the funded capacity of **${n(funded)}**. This rules out a simple "the City reports funded capacity" explanation and suggests the live dashboard draws on an operational capacity definition that is not publicly documented.`
  } else if (city < actual) {
    sentence = `The Mixed Adult Emergency row is notable: the City's dashboard shows **${n(city)}** beds, which is **${n(actual - city)}** *below* BonQuery's Actual Bed Capacity of **${n(actual)}** (and **${n(funded - city)}** below the funded capacity of **${n(funded)}**). The City's figure does not align with either CKAN definition, and the exact derivation of the live dashboard's "Actual Bed Capacity" is not publicly documented.`
  } else {
    sentence = `The Mixed Adult Emergency row sits between the BonQuery Actual Bed Capacity (**${n(actual)}**) and the funded capacity (**${n(funded)}**) — the City reports **${n(city)}**. The exact derivation of the live dashboard's "Actual Bed Capacity" is not publicly documented.`
  }
  return md`${sentence}`
}
```

### Bridging & Triage programs (not published to open data)

The City's live dashboard shows 29 people in Bridging & Triage programs on May 14, 2026. This program type is not present in the CKAN open-data export at all — the programs are tracked in the City's internal operational system but not published to the open data portal. BonQuery cannot reproduce this figure from open data, and the row is labelled accordingly in the table above.

### Retroactive updates to open data

The City of Toronto retroactively updates its CKAN open-data export. When BonQuery's automated pipeline pulls new data, it routinely detects that values for previously published dates have changed. The City does not publish a changelog or version history for these updates, so there is no public record of what was revised, when, or why.

BonQuery's daily pipeline pulls the full CKAN export twice a day and republishes the site whenever any row changes — including retroactive revisions to dates already in the dataset. Whatever appears on BonQuery's side of the comparison reflects the most recent CKAN export; whatever appears on the City's side is the one-time snapshot taken from the live dashboard on the reference date noted above.

One implication for this page: where the City has substantially revised a date's data after the City snapshot was taken, the **City − BQ Gap** in the breakdown table will widen and may flip sign. The Mixed Adult Emergency row is the clearest current example. Such gaps are driven primarily by retroactive CKAN revisions rather than by a definitional difference between the two data products — distinct from the funded-vs-actual gap, which is real operational data describing offline beds.

### The takeaway

BonQuery's table is fully reproducible by anyone from published open data: the [CKAN export](https://open.toronto.ca/dataset/daily-shelter-overnight-service-occupancy-capacity/), the aggregation function, and this comparison page together constitute a complete audit trail. The live dashboard draws on internal system state that is not fully present in the CKAN export. These are different tools for different questions: the live dashboard is designed for daily operational decisions; BonQuery's table is designed for longitudinal, reproducible research.

---

Contains information licensed under the [Open Government Licence – Toronto](https://open.toronto.ca/open-data-licence/). Source: City of Toronto Open Data — [Daily Shelter & Overnight Service Occupancy & Capacity](https://open.toronto.ca/dataset/daily-shelter-overnight-service-occupancy-capacity/). Aggregated by [BonQuery](https://bonquery.ca).

BonQuery's aggregated data is available for download from 2021 to present: [JSON](https://bonquery.ca/data/daily_occupancy.json) · [CSV](https://bonquery.ca/data/daily_occupancy.csv)
